name: Ben Jealous
subject: Live with purpose, speak with courage, resist.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friend -
Today, on the 50th on the anniversary of his assassination, we celebrate the life of **Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr**.
Martin Luther King, Jr. was a drum major for justice, for peace, for righteousness, and he left a committed life behind.
To mark the occasion, I spoke with my good friend, famed historian Taylor Branch about what today's activists can learn from Dr. King's work as we continue the fight against poverty and racism in the Trump era.
**Forward this email, and share the video below to commemorate Dr. King's legacy. **
[](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a79a/7ab0e2c4/7f53313a/349a704d/585590579/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/benjealous/videos/10155656943204436/")
**[SHARE ON FACEBOOK](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a79a/7ab0e2c4/7f53313a/349a704d/585590579/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/benjealous/videos/10155656943204436/")**
The best tribute to King's legacy is to continue his work. May his example inspire each and every one of us to live each day with purpose, speak with courage, and _resist_.
Onward,
Ben

name: The White House
subject: Immigration loopholes that put everyone at risk
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=e7c99e0e6665c0919d7749d4464c783a7723f185c46e3732f50532ae7f7d4fe14d8c14695cccc3a73e3dbb7f0ae941abc814bbaefe87ea17

Immigration loopholes that put everyone at risk

Yesterday, President Donald J. Trump ordered the National Guard to assist Border Patrol in securing our Nation’s Southwest border with Mexico. The President’s decision reflects a lack of Congressional action to reform our broken immigration system and strengthen security at the U.S.-Mexico border.

Grave loopholes in our immigration laws are hindering enforcement efforts. One of the most significant such loopholes relates to unaccompanied alien children (UACs). Many UACs are smuggled into the United States by criminal organizations, and once here, very few will be repatriated.

Most UACs are older teens from Central America. Some are MS-13 gang members who use our immigration system to infiltrate the country, but many other UACs are also at great risk from gang activity. Releasing UACs to sponsors with often unstable living situations makes these teens prime targets for gang recruitment and violence.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34de9c5956b773ea324c7b656fbe17876505d783f754e72b092515ac28899c740ce7702be0e0d7e8148ef290960e4bc5bcbcdc4c2719960c
Read more about how loopholes in trafficking laws put victims—and American citizens—at risk.

More:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34de9c5956b773eacbad7be20ca5cc5e43618aa74e2f67d466e0aa7b1bbb82192c26e310f0e8ba0d7a81225a5ad8d416e967fabd2fd86131
The basics you need to know about UAC loopholes

The President visits West Virginia

Today, President Trump travels to West Virginia for the fourth time during his Presidency, meeting with small business leaders and families who have benefited from tax cuts he signed into law in December.

Here is what the President will tell West Virginians this afternoon:

- America is open for business once again. Energy exports are at a record high and net imports are the lowest in more than 30 years.
- Wages are rising at the fastest pace in nearly a decade. A record number of small businesses say now is a good time to expand.
- These big-picture numbers are amazing, but what I want to hear is your personal stories.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34de9c5956b773ea6566dd9822c724297662d1b1236b15f49e64266cb18f7ba9508ecdbce51ff687fe05fec2db917465df4475cb8ef439bb
Watch President Trump’s visit to West Virginia live at 2 p.m. ET today.

Infrastructure plan provides skilled jobs

When President Trump released the details of his infrastructure initiative in February, one of its key pillars was the need to reinvest in American workers. Yesterday, Labor Secretary Alex Acosta explained exactly what that would look like under the President’s plan.

“The president’s plan not only invests in physical infrastructure, it also invests in workforce education,” Secretary Acosta writes. He explains that a cumbersome Federal bureaucracy too often limits Americans’ potential by restricting Federal assistance to traditional educational programs.

By embracing a wider range of skills-based learning opportunities, including apprenticeships, President Trump’s plan stands to help a much larger class of American workers.

Go deeper:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34de9c5956b773ea66bdf50132ed3957eae531d3ab5859a6ff5a845f02a520fd513780d98b2e703ecc4f8ef6c8cb42e48595dc678953ee1d
The President’s infrastructure plan makes it a great time to be a job-seeker in America

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump at the 140th White House Easter Egg Roll | April 2, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

Today, after receiving his daily intelligence briefing, President Trump will travel to White Sulphur Springs, West Virginia, to participate in a roundtable discussion about tax reform.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34de9c5956b773ea34b37e8d967de8bfe39f68582c96ef4c8ad0cfad9aeca301b7e3fbeeeeabbe01d31b2021e331e901be9261ddb245e30c

Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4707df4adbaf9122e23d3dd716a9ca022db3a3f098d67ef3228b45d4da37b256dbc332ba628a649c511a5f8c74f3dae7770a9e54e1bb11630
Unsubscribe

Friend,

UNTIL THE WALL IS BUILT, I WILL DO EVERYTHING IN MY POWER AS COMMANDER IN CHIEF TO SECURE THE BORDER.

Our weak border is a matter of **national defense.** If we lose our borders, we lose our country.

We've done enough securing other countries' borders. It's time to focus on America's.

**[But we still need to keep fighting to build the wall.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=e84cb91041d48d3e36c5952912fd5a17e9a596111bd4b6565c1c45ac00f2260a5307b30a9df579837dde9ec8b446af5efc825ffebebda1a1)**

body:
Peter,

The internet is about to slow down.

On April 23, the Trump Administration’s Federal
Communications Commission (FCC) will complete its quest to repeal
the Obama Administration’s rules to preserve net neutrality.

This could not be more important. Net neutrality is what
keeps our internet open. Without it, the big cable companies will
be able to charge people like you extra based on what you do
online and control what online content you have access to.

But there’s hope: With a simple majority vote in Congress, we
can stop the FCC from repealing these critical rules. Will you
join me in calling on my colleagues to save net neutrality?

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: Will hate win if we stay silent on this issue
body:
Peter,
You may have seen my tweet, “Hate wins.” I sent that tweet when I resigned from my position with the CMA Foundation, less than 24 hours after I joined.
I resigned after a torrent of irrational vitriol was directed at me for my religious and political views. I did so to mitigate any distraction from the core mission of the Foundation, to help kids acquire musical instruments and participate in music programs.
It wasn’t because I was afraid of the attacks. I’ve been fighting for conservative values for some time now, so I’m not surprised when I’m personally attacked.
But I am reminded of the surprising level of absolute intolerance from the left in our country – an intolerance directed towards every day Americans who believe differently than they do.
The real concern is that this intolerance is now being legislated and argued in our courts...it is an entirely new battle.
We have numerous cases where liberal judges are ruling on “intolerance.”
Jack Phillips, a Colorado Baker, refused to bake a wedding cake for a same-sex couple in 2012 because of his religious beliefs. A judge ruled against his first amendment rights and today his case awaits a decision from the Supreme Court.
The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit recently declared unconstitutional the historic Bladensburg World War I Veterans Memorial, which has stood since 1925 in honor of 49 Bladensburg-area men who died during WWI. Why? Because the memorial is shaped like a cross.
And forced “inclusion,” at the expense of religious liberty, is being introduced by state legislatures.
The California state legislature recently introduced a new bill (AB2943) that mandates acceptance of lesbian, gay bisexual, and transgender behavior to be a matter of science. This bill would actually make it illegal to counsel or help those with unwanted same-sex attractions.
There are no exemptions for churches, religious non-profits, pastors, or teachers. In fact, as it is currently written, the legislation enforces absolute inclusion on “goods and services” which could include the sale of books sold in California that encourage people to trust in Jesus Christ and the power of the Gospel to heal their sexual brokenness or same-sex attraction.
This is no longer about “tolerance.” It’s truly about the left’s intolerance of any beliefs that are contrary to their own.
But hate doesn't have to win. As Christians, we should not stand for judges who make rulings or elected leaders who pass legislation that strip us of our right to religious freedom.
If Christians stay silent, the left’s intolerance will rob us of our ability to share the truth of the gospel.
That’s why we must elect leaders who will not cave to the pressure. We need elected leaders who will stand for religious liberty by the judges they appoint and the legislation they pass.
This is what is at stake in the midterm elections this year.
On the federal level, there are 149 judicial vacancies out of 890 judicial positions. The Senate has confirmed only 29 of President Trump’s judicial nominees, which means if we don’t elect Senators who believe in religious liberty, then we have no hope of seeing good judges in our federal courts.
It’s estimated that of the 870 judicial positions, 438 judges could vacate their seats because of their senior status eligibility by the year 2020.
This means that President Trump and the Senate, **who we will elect this fall** , could possibly appoint 50.3% of lifetime appointments in Trump’s first term!
This is huge for religious liberty. It will be game-changing one way or the other. We’re already seeing extremely tight Senate Races across the country.
[Peter, I’m counting on you to help us get more like-minded Christians to the polls.](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/p180405apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180405apl01nd&n=1)
Together we can elect Senators and leaders who believe in religious liberty and we can see decisions reversed – if millions of Christians simply vote.
We must reach more like-minded Christians to vote this fall. We can’t do this without your financial support. I’m asking you to stand up against the powerful force that is seeking to strip us of religious freedom in the false name of tolerance.
[](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/p180405apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180405apl01nd&n=2)
**United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180405apl01nd&n=3)
©2018 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

A judge ruled against his first amendment rights and today his case awaits a decision from the Supreme Court. The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit recently declared unconstitutional the historic Bladensburg World War I Veterans Memorial, which has stood since 1925 in honor of 49 Bladensburg-area men who died during WWI. Why? Because the memorial is shaped like a cross. And forced “inclusion,” at the expense of religious liberty, is being introduced by state legislatures. The California state legislature recently introduced a new bill (AB2943) that mandates acceptance of lesbian, gay bisexual, and transgender behavior to be a matter of science. This bill would actually make it illegal to counsel or help those with unwanted same-sex attractions. There are no exemptions for churches, religious non-profits, pastors, or teachers. In fact, as it is currently written, the legislation enforces absolute inclusion on “goods and services” which could include the sale of books sold in California that encourage people to trust in Jesus Christ and the power of the Gospel to heal their sexual brokenness or same-sex attraction. This is no longer about “tolerance.” It’s truly about the left’s intolerance of any beliefs that are contrary to their own. But hate doesn't have to win. As Christians, we should not stand for judges who make rulings or elected leaders who pass legislation that strip us of our right to religious freedom. If Christians stay silent, the left’s intolerance will rob us of our ability to share the truth of the gospel. That’s why we must elect leaders who will not cave to the pressure. We need elected leaders who will stand for religious liberty by the judges they appoint and the legislation they pass. This is what is at stake in the midterm elections this year. On the federal level, there are 149 judicial vacancies out of 890 judicial positions. The Senate has confirmed only 29 of President Trump’s judicial nominees, which means if we don’t elect Senators who believe in religious liberty, then we have no hope of seeing good judges in our federal courts. It’s estimated that of the 870 judicial positions, 438 judges could vacate their seats because of their senior status eligibility by the year 2020. This means that President Trump and the Senate, who we will elect this fall , could possibly appoint 50.3% of lifetime appointments in Trump’s first term! This is huge for religious liberty. It will be game-changing one way or the other. We’re already seeing extremely tight Senate Races across the country. Together we can elect Senators and leaders who believe in religious liberty and we can see decisions reversed – if millions of Christians simply vote. We must reach more like-minded Christians to vote this fall. We can’t do this without your financial support. I’m asking you to stand up against the powerful force that is seeking to strip us of religious freedom in the false name of tolerance. United. We Stand. Gov. Mike Huckabee Honorary National Chairman ©2018 My Faith Votes 10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134
